stories.title: Nato says 'no provision' to expel members after report US could seek to suspend Spain
stories.body: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish allies over a perceived lack of support for Iran war.

stories.title: No 10 says Falklands sovereignty rests with UK after report of US 'review'
stories.body: An internal Pentagon document reportedly raised the prospect of a change in position in retaliation for the UK not joining the Iran war.

stories.title: Who is making decisions in Iran?
stories.body: Iran's new supreme leader is supposed to have the final say, but in practice the picture is far murkier.

stories.title: Three Kosovo Serbs jailed over deadly gun battle and monastery siege
stories.body: Kosovo's court sentences two men to life imprisonment and another to a 30-year term for the 2023 attack.

stories.title: US justice department drops probe into Fed chairman Jerome Powell
stories.body: President Donald Trump had accused Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed's building.

stories.title: Mandelson under formal investigation by EU's anti-fraud office
stories.body: The EU's anti-fraud office confirms it has "sufficient information" to launch an investigation, but does not say if it involves fraud.

stories.title: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre during Syria civil war arrested
stories.body: Amjad Youssef was wanted over the mass killing of blindfolded civilians in April 2013 in the district of Damascus.

stories.title: Israeli police investigate after officers 'cut Palestinian flag' from skullcap
stories.body: The British Israeli man says he was detained after someone took offence at his kippah embroidered with Israeli and Palestinian flags.

stories.title: Pirates hijack oil tanker off the coast of Somalia
stories.body: Piracy, once notorious in this stretch of the Indian Ocean, has made a comeback in recent years.

stories.title: Poisoning suspected in deaths of 18 wolves in Italian national park
stories.body: After the carcasses of 10 wolves were found last week, another eight have been found in recent days.

stories.title: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu treated for early-stage prostate cancer
stories.body: The Israeli prime minister says he is "in excellent physical condition" after having a malignant tumour removed.

stories.title: Epstein housed abuse victims in London flats, BBC reveals
stories.body: The revelations intensify concerns about repeated police decisions not to investigate 2015 trafficking claims.

stories.title: Kenyan leader sparks uproar after mocking Nigerians' spoken English
stories.body: William Ruto says Kenyans speak "some of the best English in the world" while it is hard to understand Nigerians.

stories.title: South Africa's top envoy to Ghana summoned over attacks on foreigners
stories.body: A Ghanaian national was confronted by South Africans in a viral clip and questioned about his immigration status.

stories.title: South Korea police arrest man for posting AI photo of runaway wolf
stories.body: The widely circulated image had prompted authorities to move their search operation.

stories.title: Behind Nigeria's murky coup plot - the money, the prayers and a Nollywood arrest
stories.body: What court papers and sources close to the investigation have told the BBC about the scope of the plan.

stories.title: Steve Rosenberg: Kremlin's tightening grip on internet fuels Russian discontent
stories.body: Officials say the restrictions are for public safety, but businesses and the public are feeling the impact.

stories.title: Relatives of 10 scientists who died or vanished grapple with impact of wild speculation
stories.body: Conspiracies are running rampant online after the deaths or disappearances of at least 10 people tied to sensitive US research.

stories.title: Headscarf with a beret: Muslim designers showcase floral dresses and boxy streetwear in Paris
stories.body: Some young attendees say the event demonstrated a more inclusive French culture taking shape.

stories.title: A rumour, a lynching in India and a long wait for justice
stories.body: A court has convicted 20 people for the 2018 mob lynching of two men falsely accused of kidnapping children.

root_causes.name: Geopolitical instability due to unilateral military actions
root_causes.rationale: The US's perceived retaliation against allies for lack of support in the Iran war and murky leadership in Iran suggest escalating tensions that could destabilize international relations.
root_causes.intervention: Diplomatic engagement and multilateral conflict resolution mechanisms to address grievances and prevent escalation.
root_causes.prediction: Increased geopolitical fragmentation with potential for new alliances forming outside NATO by mid-2027.
root_causes.falsification: If NATO members reaffirm unity and no significant shifts in alliances occur by mid-2027.

root_causes.name: Resurgence of piracy and maritime insecurity
root_causes.rationale: Piracy off the Somali coast indicates a breakdown in regional security and governance, potentially disrupting global trade routes.
root_causes.intervention: Strengthen international naval patrols and invest in local governance and economic development in affected regions.
root_causes.prediction: Increase in piracy incidents and ransom demands by 2028 if interventions are not implemented.
root_causes.falsification: If piracy incidents decline significantly by 2028 due to effective interventions.

root_causes.name: Environmental degradation and wildlife poisoning
root_causes.rationale: The poisoning of wolves in Italy highlights systemic issues in wildlife protection and environmental enforcement.
root_causes.intervention: Implement stricter regulations and monitoring systems for wildlife protection and investigate the root causes of poisoning incidents.
root_causes.prediction: Further wildlife poisoning incidents in Europe by 2027 if regulations are not strengthened.
root_causes.falsification: If no significant wildlife poisoning incidents occur in Europe by 2027.

root_causes.name: Social polarization and xenophobia
root_causes.rationale: Incidents of xenophobia in South Africa and Kenya's leader mocking Nigerians' English highlight growing social tensions and polarization.
root_causes.intervention: Promote cross-cultural dialogue and education campaigns to reduce xenophobia and foster social cohesion.
root_causes.prediction: Increased xenophobic incidents and social unrest in Africa by 2028 if interventions are not implemented.
root_causes.falsification: If xenophobic incidents decline and social cohesion improves by 2028.

root_causes.name: Erosion of trust in institutions and conspiracy theories
root_causes.rationale: Speculation around the deaths or disappearances of scientists tied to sensitive research reflects growing distrust in institutions and susceptibility to conspiracy theories.
root_causes.intervention: Increase transparency in sensitive research and strengthen public communication strategies to counter misinformation.
root_causes.prediction: Proliferation of conspiracy theories and public distrust in scientific institutions by 2027.
root_causes.falsification: If public trust in institutions improves and conspiracy theories decline by 2027.

predictions.prediction: Increased geopolitical fragmentation with potential for new alliances forming outside NATO.
predictions.falsification: If NATO members reaffirm unity and no significant shifts in alliances occur by mid-2027.

predictions.prediction: Increase in piracy incidents and ransom demands.

predictions.falsification: If xenophobic incidents decline and social cohesion improves by 2028.

predictions.falsification: If public trust in institutions improves and conspiracy theories decline by 2027.

narrative: The global landscape in 2026 is marked by escalating geopolitical tensions, environmental challenges, social polarization, and a growing erosion of trust in institutions. The US's unilateral actions in the Iran war have strained alliances, while piracy resurges in the Indian Ocean, threatening maritime security. Environmental degradation is evident in wildlife poisoning incidents, and xenophobia is fueling social unrest in Africa. Meanwhile, conspiracy theories surrounding sensitive research highlight the fragility of public trust in institutions. Addressing these systemic root causes requires coordinated interventions, including diplomatic engagement, strengthened regulations, cross-cultural dialogue, and transparency in governance.
